bullet_points: Implement GPU-accelerated pseudo-spectral 2D NS solver in vorticity-streamfunction form on N=1024 grid using PyTorch (device='cuda'), with torch.fft.rfft2/irfft2
Use integrating factor (IF) method for hyperviscosity with nu_h=1e-19, p=4 (8th-order); RK4 on nonlinear term only
Forcing: stochastic white-in-time at k∈[3,5] (NOT k∈[10,12] — use Simulation Design values), calibrate force_amp empirically to achieve epsilon_inj=0.1
Wavenumber convention: integer wavenumbers via torch.fft.fftfreq(N, d=1.0/N); de-alias with 2/3 rule
Spinup: run until T/T_L >= 10, saving diagnostics only; monitor k_peak and T_L
Production: run for T_prod = 50 * T_L_spinup_end; seed N_tracers=5000 uniformly at start of production; save initial tracer positions to tracer_initial_positions.npy for reproducibility in Step 5

Tracer advection: GPU grid_sample bilinear interpolation at every tracer snapshot step with dt_snap=0.05 (NOT 0.01); record positions and velocities; enforce periodic boundary conditions via modulo 2π
Save Eulerian velocity/vorticity snapshots coarsened 1024→256 via AvgPool2d(4) at dt_vel=2.0
Save energy spectrum E(k,t) at every tracer snapshot using integer wavenumber bins (k=1,2,...,N//2) with azimuthal averaging; k_peak defined as argmax E(k) excluding k=0
Save diagnostics structured array with fields (time, E_total, E_rms, omega_rms, T_L, k_peak, d_vv_estimate)
NaN/Inf guard after each step; verify U_rms*dt_snap < 0.1 before production
Write all output files to /home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/data/ with exact shapes specified
Save sim_params.json with all key parameters including N, nu_h, epsilon_inj, dt_snap, dt_vel, T_spinup, T_prod, T_L_at_start_of_production
sub_tasks.sub_task: DNS simulation — 2D Navier-Stokes solver with tracer advection

bullet_points: Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, energy_spectrum.npy, diagnostics.npy
Compute mean tracer displacement per snapshot step using periodic-aware distance (wrap differences to [-π, π]); verify < 0.3 * 2π ≈ 1.88; print result and flag if exceeded

Compute VACF C_vv(τ) = ⟨v(t)·v(t+τ)⟩ / ⟨v(t)·v(t)⟩ averaged over all tracers and all reference times; evaluate at lags τ = 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 steps; verify C_vv(τ=1) > 0.3; report decorrelation time τ_corr as first lag where C_vv drops below 1/e
Plot E(k) at t = 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 1.0 × T_prod on a single figure with overlaid curves; confirm k_peak drifts from ~4 toward ~1-2
Print all key statistics (mean displacement, VACF values at each lag, τ_corr, k_peak at each time fraction) to console
Save verification plot to data directory
sub_tasks.sub_task: Data quality verification and inverse cascade validation

bullet_points: Load tracer positions, velocities, times, and diagnostics
Define overlapping windows of width W=8*T_L, step ΔW=2*T_L; compute ~26 windows

For each window: compute net displacement magnitudes |r(t+τ) - r(t)| at lags τ={0.5,1,2}*T_L; treat x and y components separately then pool to maximize sample size; exclude windows with fewer than 500 displacement samples per lag

Fit α via McCulloch quantile method: compute ν_α = (q95-q5)/(q75-q25); implement hardcoded McCulloch (1986) lookup table with bilinear interpolation in the (ν_α, ν_β) plane; clip α to [1.0, 2.0] after interpolation
Also compute Hill estimator α_Hill as cross-check using top-k order statistics
For each window: fit MSD(τ) power law to get γ(t); fit VACF algebraic decay to get ν(t)
Extract d_vv(t) and k_peak(t) from diagnostics for each window center
Run ADF and KPSS stationarity tests on α(t), γ(t), ν(t) time series; print test statistics and p-values
Fit α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c using nonlinear least squares; print fitted parameters with uncertainties
Save all window statistics (alpha_mcculloch, alpha_hill, gamma, nu, d_vv, k_peak, window_centers, adf_results, kpss_results) to npz file
Print all fitted parameters, stationarity test p-values, and key statistics to console
sub_tasks.sub_task: Non-stationarity analysis of Lévy exponents across overlapping time windows

bullet_points: Load saved window statistics from Step 3
Generate multi-panel figure: α(t) with both McCulloch and Hill estimates shown, γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t), d_vv(t) all vs time window center
Generate scatter plot of α vs k_peak/k_box with fitted curve α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c overlaid; include confidence band
Annotate plots with ADF/KPSS test p-values and stationarity conclusions
Save plots to data directory
Load velocity_snapshots.npy as memory-mapped array (not fully into RAM); load vel_times.npy
Apply 2D DWT using db4 wavelet (periodic boundary mode) to each velocity snapshot one at a time to avoid OOM; extract detail coefficients at levels j=1,...,6 using PyWavelets

Compute Okubo-Weiss parameter Q from 256×256 velocity snapshots using finite differences; compute masking threshold once from time-averaged |Q| field (75th percentile); apply mask Q < -Q_threshold when computing spatial averages for T_eddy

For each level j: compute wavelet energy E_j(x,y,t) = sum_d |D_j^d(x,y,t)|²; compute temporal autocorrelation C_E(τ,j) averaged over masked pixels; integrate until C_E < 0.1 (max lag = min(T_prod/4, 50*dt_vel)) to get T_eddy(j)
Reconstruct filtered velocity fields ũ_j for j_min ∈ {1,2,3,4} by zeroing detail coefficients below j_min; store as memory-mapped arrays or process in chunks

Re-advect 5000 tracers seeded at positions from tracer_initial_positions.npy through each filtered field using GPU-accelerated RK4; use vel_times.npy for temporal interpolation weights (not assuming uniform spacing); enforce periodic boundary wrapping via modulo 2π before each grid_sample call
For each filtered field: compute α(j) via McCulloch method, γ_MSD(j) from MSD power law, ν_VACF(j) from VACF decay
Save T_eddy(j), α(j), γ_MSD(j), ν_VACF(j) for all j to npz file
Print all T_eddy values, α(j), γ(j), ν(j) to console
sub_tasks.sub_task: Wavelet decomposition, eddy-lifetime estimation, and filtered tracer re-advection

bullet_points: Load wavelet results from Step 5 and window statistics from Step 3
Extract β(j) from α(j) via inverse relation β = 2*(α(j)-1)/α(j) (derived from α = 1+β/(2-β)); fit log(β) vs log(T_eddy) via linear regression to obtain scaling exponent δ; print δ with uncertainty
Plot α(j) vs T_eddy(j): show data points, direct power-law fit to α, and CTRW-predicted curve from β power law overlaid for visual comparison; annotate with δ
Plot γ_MSD(j) and ν_VACF(j) vs T_eddy(j) on separate panels
Synthesis panel: compare α(t) from Step 3 with α(j_peak(t)) from wavelet analysis at dominant scale j corresponding to k_peak(t) at each window center
Generate 2D heatmap of α as function of (T_eddy, k_peak/k_box) using all available data points from both analyses
Save all synthesis plots to data directory
sub_tasks.sub_task: Plot wavelet scaling results and synthesis

bullet_points: Summarize DNS simulation parameters (N=1024, k_force∈[3,5], nu_h=1e-19, epsilon_inj=0.1, dt_snap=0.05) and spinup/production run outcomes (T_L value, k_peak evolution, U_rms)
Report data quality verification with numerical values: mean displacement per step vs threshold 1.88, VACF values at lags 1,2,4,8,16 steps, τ_corr value, and whether C_vv(τ=1) > 0.3 criterion was met
Describe inverse cascade development quantitatively: k_peak values at t=0.1,0.3,0.6,1.0×T_prod

Report non-stationarity analysis with full numerical results: α(t) range and mean, γ(t) range, ν(t) range; ADF test statistics and p-values for each series; KPSS test statistics and p-values; fitted parameters a, b, c with confidence intervals for α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c; R² of fit

Report wavelet decomposition results: T_eddy(j) for j=1,...,6; α(j), γ(j), ν(j) for each filtered field; fitted scaling exponent δ with uncertainty; R² of β vs T_eddy fit; whether CTRW prediction α = 1+β/(2-β) is supported
Synthesis: quantitative comparison of α(t) vs α(j_peak(t)); interpretation of 2D α map; conclusion on stationarity of Lévy exponent
Quantitative comparison against prior v1 result α ≈ 1.4 ± 0.1: report whether v2 α values fall within or outside this band, and discuss physical interpretation of any differences
sub_tasks.sub_task: Write full Results section of the paper
